verses.latin_vulgate: In anno quo ingressus est Thathan in Azotum, cum misisset eum Sargon, rex Assyriorum, et pugnasset contra Azotum, et cepisset eam :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בִּשְׁנַ֨ת בֹּ֤א תַרְתָּן֙ אַשְׁדּ֔וֹדָה בִּשְׁלֹ֣חַ אֹת֔וֹ סַֽרְג֖וֹן מֶ֣לֶךְ אַשּׁ֑וּר וַיִּלָּ֥חֶם בְּאַשְׁדּ֖וֹד וַֽיִּלְכְּדָֽהּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: In the year that Tharthan entered into Azotus, when Sargon the king of the Assyrians had sent him, and he had fought against Azotus, and had taken it:
verses.finnish_biblia: Sinä vuonna, kuin Tartan tuli Asdodiin, jonka Assyrian kuningas Sargon oli lähettänyt, ja soti Asdodia vastaan ja voitti sen:
verses.king_james: In the year that Tartan came unto Ashdod, (when Sargon the king of Assyria sent him,) and fought against Ashdod, and took it;

verses.latin_vulgate: in tempore illo locutus est Dominus in manu Isaiæ, filii Amos, dicens : Vade, et solve saccum de lumbis tuis, et calceamenta tua tolle de pedibus tuis. Et fecit sic, vadens nudus et discalceatus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בָּעֵ֣ת הַהִ֗יא דִּבֶּ֣ר יְהֹוָה֮ בְּיַ֣ד יְשַֽׁעְיָ֣הוּ בֶן־אָמוֹץ֮ לֵאמֹר֒ לֵ֗ךְ וּפִתַּחְתָּ֤ הַשַּׂק֙ מֵעַ֣ל מׇתְנֶ֔יךָ וְנַעַלְךָ֥ תַחֲלֹ֖ץ מֵעַ֣ל רַגְלֶ֑ךָ וַיַּ֣עַשׂ כֵּ֔ן הָלֹ֖ךְ עָר֥וֹם וְיָחֵֽף׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: At that same time the Lord spoke by the hand of Isaiah the son of Amos, saying Go, and loose the sackcloth from off thy loins, and take off thy shoes from thy feet. And he did so, and went naked, and barefoot.
verses.finnish_biblia: Silloin puhui Herra Jesaian Amotsin pojan kautta ja sanoi: mene ja riisu säkki kupeistas ja kengät jaloistas: ja hän teki niin, ja kävi alasti ja paljain jaloin.
verses.king_james: At the same time spake the Lord by Isaiah the son of Amoz, saying, Go and loose the sackcloth from off thy loins, and put off thy shoe from thy foot. And he did so, walking naked and barefoot.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit Dominus :   Sicut ambulavit servus meus Isaias nudus et discalceatus,  trium annorum signum et portentum erit  super Ægyptum et super Æthiopiam ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר יְהֹוָ֔ה כַּאֲשֶׁ֥ר הָלַ֛ךְ עַבְדִּ֥י יְשַֽׁעְיָ֖הוּ עָר֣וֹם וְיָחֵ֑ף שָׁלֹ֤שׁ שָׁנִים֙ א֣וֹת וּמוֹפֵ֔ת עַל־מִצְרַ֖יִם וְעַל־כּֽוּשׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the Lord said: As my servant Isaiah hath walked, naked and barefoot, it shall be a sign and a wonder of three years upon Egypt, and upon Ethiopia,
verses.finnish_biblia: Sanoi siis Herra: niinkuin palveliani Jesaia käy alasti ja paljailla jaloilla, kolmen ajastajan merkiksi ja ihmeeksi Egyptille ja Etiopialle:
verses.king_james: And the Lord said, Like as my servant Isaiah hath walked naked and barefoot three years for a sign and wonder upon Egypt and upon Ethiopia;

verses.latin_vulgate: sic minabit rex Assyriorum captivitatem Ægypti,  et transmigrationem Æthiopiæ,  juvenum et senum, nudam et discalceatam, discoopertis natibus, ad ignominiam Ægypti.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֵּ֣ן יִנְהַ֣ג מֶֽלֶךְ־אַ֠שּׁ֠וּר אֶת־שְׁבִ֨י מִצְרַ֜יִם וְאֶת־גָּל֥וּת כּ֛וּשׁ נְעָרִ֥ים וּזְקֵנִ֖ים עָר֣וֹם וְיָחֵ֑ף וַחֲשׂוּפַ֥י שֵׁ֖ת עֶרְוַ֥ת מִצְרָֽיִם׃
verses.douay_rheims: So shall the king of the Assyrians lead away the prisoners of Egypt, and the captivity of Ethiopia, young and old, naked and barefoot, with their buttocks uncovered to the shame of Egypt.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin on Assyrian kuningas ajava vangittua Egyptiä ja hävitettyä Etiopiaa, sekä nuoria että vanhoja, alastomia ja paljasjalkaisia, paljastetun hävyn kanssa, Egyptille hävyksi.
verses.king_james: So shall the king of Assyria lead away the Egyptians prisoners, and the Ethiopians captives, young and old, naked and barefoot, even with their buttocks uncovered, to the shame of Egypt.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et timebunt, et confundentur  ab Æthiopia spe sua,  et ab Ægypto gloria sua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְחַתּ֖וּ וָבֹ֑שׁוּ מִכּוּשׁ֙ מַבָּטָ֔ם וּמִן־מִצְרַ֖יִם תִּפְאַרְתָּֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they shall be afraid, and ashamed of Ethiopia their hope, and of Egypt their glory.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja heidän pitää peljästymän ja häpeemän Etiopiaa, johon he luottivat, ja Egyptiläisiä, joista he kerskasivat.
verses.king_james: And they shall be afraid and ashamed of Ethiopia their expectation, and of Egypt their glory.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dicet habitator insulæ hujus in die illa :  Ecce hæc erat spes nostra,  ad quos confugimus in auxilium, ut liberarent nos  a facie regis Assyriorum :  et quomodo effugere poterimus nos ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְ֠אָמַ֠ר יֹשֵׁ֨ב הָאִ֣י הַזֶּה֮ בַּיּ֣וֹם הַהוּא֒ הִנֵּה־כֹ֣ה מַבָּטֵ֗נוּ אֲשֶׁר־נַ֤סְנוּ שָׁם֙ לְעֶזְרָ֔ה לְהִ֨נָּצֵ֔ל מִפְּנֵ֖י מֶ֣לֶךְ אַשּׁ֑וּר וְאֵ֖יךְ נִמָּלֵ֥ט אֲנָֽחְנוּ׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And the inhabitants of this isle shall say in that day: Lo this was our hope, to whom we fled for help, to deliver us from the face of the king of the Assyrians: and how shall we be able to escape?
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja tämän luodon asuvaiset pitää silloin sanoman: onko tämä meidän turvamme, johon olemme paenneet apua saamaan, että me Assyrian kuninkaalta pelastettaisiin? kuinka me olemme päässeet?
verses.king_james: And the inhabitant of this isle shall say in that day, Behold, such is our expectation, whither we flee for help to be delivered from the king of Assyria: and how shall we escape?
